title: The One Where Underdog Gets Away

jokes.text: Rachel, Rachel, sweetheart! You're a terrible, terrible waitress. Really, really awful.
jokes.setup: Rachel asks for an advance on her salary
jokes.punchline: Terry's blunt, repetitive assessment of her terrible waitressing
jokes.explanation: Solid character-based humor establishing Rachel's incompetence as a waitress. The repetition works well.

jokes.text: I hear what you're saying. I'm with you.
jokes.setup: Terry just called her terrible at her job
jokes.punchline: Rachel's completely inappropriate agreement with the harsh criticism
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Rachel's privileged disconnect from consequences. Great economy of words.

jokes.text: Does anybody need coffee? -Yeah, over here.
jokes.setup: Rachel claims she's doing better as a waitress
jokes.punchline: Immediate proof she's not paying attention to customers needing service
jokes.explanation: Classic sitcom irony - claim immediately contradicted by reality. Well-structured timing.

jokes.text: Only 98.50 to go!
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to earn money for Vail trip
jokes.punchline: Pathetically small progress after begging customers for advance tips
jokes.explanation: Shows Rachel's desperation and naivety about money in a relatable way.

jokes.text: Yes, I am. As of today, I'm officially Joey Tribbiani, actor/model.
jokes.setup: Joey is asked if he's wearing makeup
jokes.punchline: His proud announcement of being an 'actor/model'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey character moment - vain, earnest, and slightly delusional about his career.

jokes.text: That's funny. I was thinking you look more like, Joey Tribbiani, man/woman.
jokes.setup: Joey's proud actor/model announcement
jokes.punchline: Chandler's twist on the slash format to mock Joey's makeup
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that cleverly subverts Joey's format. Classic Chandler wit with perfect timing and structure.

jokes.text: No, but I hear Lyme disease is open, so you know....
jokes.setup: Discussion about what disease Joey will model for
jokes.punchline: Joey's casual, hopeful tone about getting a serious disease
jokes.explanation: Dark humor perfectly delivered through Joey's oblivious optimism. The casual 'so you know...' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Good luck, man. I hope you get it.
jokes.setup: Joey hoping to get Lyme disease modeling job
jokes.punchline: Ross sincerely wishing Joey gets a terrible disease
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that extends the dark humor of the previous joke.

jokes.text: They're not actually supposed to... I'll work on the lumps.
jokes.setup: Ross asks if she'll make mashed potatoes with lumps like Mom's
jokes.punchline: Monica's perfectionist nature conflicting with family tradition
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Monica's cooking perfectionism vs. family expectations.

jokes.text: And her boyfriend. We're celebrating Thanksgiving in December because he's lunar.
jokes.setup: Asked about Thanksgiving plans with grandma
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of boyfriend being 'lunar'
jokes.explanation: Pure Phoebe - bizarre explanation delivered matter-of-factly. 'Lunar' is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Rach, are you gonna make it to Vail? -Absolutely. Shoop, shoop, shoop.
jokes.setup: Question about Rachel's Vail plans
jokes.punchline: Rachel's confident 'shoop, shoop, shoop' despite having no money
jokes.explanation: Establishes Rachel's skiing sound effect that becomes a running gag. Shows her optimistic delusion.

jokes.text: Yeah, well, it was, but I... I broke a cup, so....
jokes.setup: Chandler questions why her debt increased
jokes.punchline: Rachel's sheepish admission of breaking something expensive
jokes.explanation: Shows Rachel's continued incompetence and the mounting obstacles to her goal.

jokes.text: ...ooh, ooh, lesbian life partner.
jokes.setup: Monica suggests inviting Carol
jokes.punchline: Ross's uncomfortable, formal way of referring to Susan
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ross's discomfort with the situation through overly formal language.

jokes.text: Well, not mine, but....
jokes.setup: Ross says he came to pick up his skull
jokes.punchline: Quick clarification that it's not actually his skull
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection joke with good timing. Ross's awkward clarification is in character.

jokes.text: Kind of like a big face without skin. -Yes. I'm familiar with the concept.
jokes.setup: Ross describes what a skull looks like
jokes.punchline: Susan's dry response to the obvious description
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response to Ross's unnecessarily detailed explanation.

jokes.text: Well, you know, you have to take a course. Otherwise they don't let you do it.
jokes.setup: Ross comments on their lesbian books
jokes.punchline: Susan's matter-of-fact joke about needing lesbian certification
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of expectations with perfect deadpan delivery. Turns stereotyping on its head.

jokes.text: Wouldn't that mean you're crazy?
jokes.setup: Susan explains she reads to the unborn baby
jokes.punchline: Ross's blunt assessment of talking to a fetus
jokes.explanation: Ross's scientific skepticism clashing with parenting instincts creates character-based humor.

jokes.text: But, we just refer to you as 'Bobo the Sperm Guy.'
jokes.setup: Ross asks if they talk about him to the baby
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous nickname they use for Ross
jokes.explanation: Brilliant punchline that's both absurd and cutting. 'Bobo the Sperm Guy' is perfectly ridiculous and memorable.

jokes.text: But you put your head inside this turkey... and we'll all talk and you'll hear everything we say.
jokes.setup: Monica wants to demonstrate that babies can hear in the womb
jokes.punchline: Her suggestion to put Ross's head in a turkey as a demonstration
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd visual that perfectly captures Monica's scientific approach to everything. Highly original and memorable.

jokes.text: In fact, I'd very much like to butter your head.
jokes.setup: The turkey head experiment is proposed
jokes.punchline: Joey's enthusiastic offer to butter Ross's head
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey response - missing the point but enthusiastic about the food aspect. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: Forget shoop, shoop, shoop.
jokes.setup: Rachel realizes she can't afford Vail
jokes.punchline: Sadly abandoning her skiing sound effect
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Rachel's deflation through abandoning her hopeful sound effect.

jokes.text: No. Here's your mail! -Thanks. You can put it on the table! -Would you just open it!
jokes.setup: Monica trying to get Rachel to notice something special about the mail
jokes.punchline: Monica's increasing frustration as Rachel misses the hints
jokes.explanation: Classic escalation comedy with Monica's mounting frustration at Rachel's obliviousness.

jokes.text: Chandler, got your traditional holiday feast. Tomato soup... grilled-cheese fixings and a family-size bag of Funyuns.
jokes.setup: Monica describing Chandler's Thanksgiving dinner
jokes.punchline: The pathetic contrast between 'traditional feast' and junk food
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Chandler's anti-holiday stance through his deliberately pathetic meal choice.

jokes.text: Very difficult to enjoy Thanksgiving dinner once you've seen it in reverse.
jokes.setup: Chandler's story about learning of his parents' divorce while eating pumpkin pie
jokes.punchline: His clinical description of vomiting up Thanksgiving dinner
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with perfect Chandler-style clinical detachment. The euphemism 'in reverse' is brilliantly understated.

jokes.text: This isn't a yam. Yam comes in purple cans. They don't come in these.
jokes.setup: Monica shows Rachel a real yam
jokes.punchline: Rachel's confusion between real vegetables and canned food
jokes.explanation: Perfect Rachel moment showing her privileged upbringing and complete disconnect from real cooking.

jokes.text: Ross, don't take this the wrong way or anything, but back off.
jokes.setup: Ross has been interfering with Monica's cooking
jokes.punchline: Monica's fake-polite way of telling Ross to leave her alone
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Monica's passive-aggressive approach to her controlling kitchen behavior.

jokes.text: Aramis. Aramis. Aramis. -Yeah, right!
jokes.setup: Joey and Kristin recognize each other from perfume counter work
jokes.punchline: Joey's enthusiastic recreation of his cologne spraying technique
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy moment that establishes Joey's department store background and his enthusiasm.

jokes.text: You're amazing! You know when to spritz. You know when to lay back.
jokes.setup: Joey praising Kristin's perfume counter technique
jokes.punchline: Treating cologne spraying like high art with serious technique analysis
jokes.explanation: Funny escalation of treating a mundane retail job like serious craft. Very Joey to be earnest about this.

jokes.text: Nothing.
jokes.setup: Joey asks what perfume she's wearing
jokes.punchline: She's not wearing any perfume, just her natural scent
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection joke that subverts the perfume counter context. Economy is perfect.

jokes.text: Set another place for Thanksgiving. My entire family thinks I have VD.
jokes.setup: Joey returns unexpectedly from his modeling shoot
jokes.punchline: Reveals his family saw him in VD poster and drew wrong conclusion
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff to the earlier disease modeling setup. Perfect Joey predicament with great economy.

jokes.text: Tonight, on a very special Blossom.
jokes.setup: Joey's dramatic announcement about his family thinking he has VD
jokes.punchline: Chandler comparing it to a cheesy TV show trope
jokes.explanation: Great meta-reference that perfectly captures the melodramatic tone of Joey's situation. Classic Chandler.

jokes.text: Cider's mulling, turkey's turkeying, yams are yamming....
jokes.setup: Monica describing her cooking progress
jokes.punchline: Making up verbs for cooking processes with enthusiastic alliteration
jokes.explanation: Playful wordplay that shows Monica's excitement about cooking. The made-up verbs are charming.

jokes.text: Get out and stop annoying me! -Oh, that's closer.
jokes.setup: Ross says it doesn't feel like Mom's cooking without her yelling
jokes.punchline: Monica yells at him, and he appreciates the authenticity
jokes.explanation: Nice callback structure where Monica inadvertently gives Ross exactly what he wanted. Good family dynamic humor.

jokes.text: Five hours from now, shoop, shoop, shoop!
jokes.setup: Rachel excited about her flight
jokes.punchline: Return of the skiing sound effect with time specificity
jokes.explanation: Another callback to the running gag, showing Rachel's renewed optimism now that she has ticket money.

jokes.text: Look out! Incoming pumpkin pie!
jokes.setup: Monica has been throwing food at Chandler
jokes.punchline: Another food projectile with dramatic announcement
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy that shows Monica's playful side. The dramatic announcement makes it funnier.

jokes.text: We laughed when you did it with the stuffing, but that's not funny.
jokes.setup: Monica threw another food item at Chandler
jokes.punchline: Ross's serious critique of Monica's food-throwing comedy timing
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Ross taking Monica's antics too seriously, like a comedy critic.

jokes.text: I mean, it's bad enough I can't be with my family because of my disease.
jokes.setup: Joey complaining about Thanksgiving arrangements
jokes.punchline: Referring to his fake VD as 'my disease' with genuine pathos
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Joey treating his fake situation like a real tragedy. Perfect Joey response.

jokes.text: Tonight's potatoes will be mashed, with lumps and in the form of Tots.
jokes.setup: Everyone wants different potato preparations
jokes.punchline: Monica's frustrated attempt to satisfy everyone's ridiculous demands
jokes.explanation: Shows Monica's people-pleasing tendency escalating to absurdity. The specificity makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Okay, Mom never hit.
jokes.setup: Monica presumably makes a threatening gesture
jokes.punchline: Ross's matter-of-fact observation about their mother's discipline style
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat that implies Monica was being threatening. Ross's clinical response is funny.

jokes.text: Because then they'd be just like my mom used to make it, before she died.
jokes.setup: Asked why she wants to add peas and onions to potatoes
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of her mother's death as reasoning
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - dropping heavy information casually. The dark humor is expertly handled.

jokes.text: An unbelievable thing happened! Underdog got away!
jokes.setup: Joey bursts in with urgent news
jokes.punchline: His excitement about a parade balloon escaping
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the episode's central plot device. Joey's childlike excitement is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: The balloon? -No, no, the actual cartoon character.
jokes.setup: Monica clarifies it's just the balloon
jokes.punchline: Joey's deadpan suggestion it might be the actual cartoon dog
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection that plays with Joey's childlike understanding of reality. Well-structured joke.

jokes.text: An 80-foot dog over the city? How often does that happen? -Almost never.
jokes.setup: Joey's enthusiastic question about the rarity of giant balloon dogs
jokes.punchline: Chandler's understated response to the obvious
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler deadpan response to Joey's obvious observation. Great timing and character work.

jokes.text: There is one way that offers a certain acoustical advantage, but....
jokes.setup: Ross about to talk to the baby through Carol's belly
jokes.punchline: His scientific euphemism for a more intimate positioning
jokes.explanation: Good innuendo handled with Ross's characteristic scientific terminology. Stays tasteful while being funny.

jokes.text: You know, I can't do this. This is... It's too weird. I feel stupid.
jokes.setup: Ross positioned to talk to the unborn baby
jokes.punchline: His awkwardness about the intimate parenting moment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ross's discomfort with the non-scientific aspects of parenting.

jokes.text: We're waiting for you to open the door. You have the keys. -No, I don't.
jokes.setup: Group locked out, expecting Joey to open door
jokes.punchline: Joey doesn't actually have the keys despite earlier implication
jokes.explanation: Sets up the central crisis of being locked out. Standard sitcom complication but well-executed.

jokes.text: No, I didn't. I asked, 'Got the keys?' -No, no, no. You said, 'Got the keys!'
jokes.setup: Argument about what Joey said about having keys
jokes.punchline: The punctuation/intonation difference between question and statement
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay joke about how punctuation/tone changes meaning. Well-structured escalating argument.

jokes.text: And everyone's telling me, 'You gotta pick a major.' So on a dare, I picked paleontology.
jokes.setup: Ross talking to the unborn baby about his career choice
jokes.punchline: Revealing he chose his profession on a dare rather than passion
jokes.explanation: Good character revelation that adds depth to Ross while being funny. The casualness makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You're happy you don't have gills anymore.
jokes.setup: Ross continuing his awkward conversation with the fetus
jokes.punchline: His bizarre attempt at baby-appropriate conversation topics
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd line that shows Ross applying his scientific knowledge inappropriately. Very original.

jokes.text: Here we come, walking down the street, get the funniest looks from everyone we meet. Hey, hey
jokes.setup: Ross reluctantly agreeing to sing to the baby
jokes.punchline: Singing the Monkees theme song to an unborn child
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing Ross warming up to fatherhood through familiar pop culture.

jokes.text: Hey, hey, you're my baby and I can't wait to meet you. When you come out I'll buy you a bagel then we'll go to the zoo
jokes.setup: Ross getting into singing to the baby
jokes.punchline: His improvised, sweetly mundane promises to his unborn child
jokes.explanation: Touching and funny character development. The specific, mundane promises make it both sweet and comedic.

jokes.text: Hey, hey, I'm your daddy, I'm the one without any breasts
jokes.setup: Ross continuing his improvised song
jokes.punchline: His awkward way of distinguishing himself from Carol and Susan
jokes.explanation: Brilliant awkward line that captures Ross's situation perfectly. Funny, touching, and completely in character.

jokes.text: This is great. It's 5:00. My plane is pulling away from the gate.
jokes.setup: Rachel stuck on the roof while her flight leaves
jokes.punchline: Her sarcastic reaction to missing her Thanksgiving plans
jokes.explanation: Standard escalation of Rachel's bad luck. The sarcasm works for her character.

jokes.text: Tomorrow is not Thanksgiving!
jokes.setup: Suggestion that Rachel could fly tomorrow instead
jokes.punchline: Rachel's frustrated statement of the obvious
jokes.explanation: Shows Rachel's attachment to traditions and her privileged perspective on holidays.

jokes.text: I got one keyhole and a zillion keys! You do the math.
jokes.setup: Asked if she can go faster with the keys
jokes.punchline: Monica's frustrated explanation of the mathematical impossibility
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Monica's stress. The 'you do the math' is a nice touch.

jokes.text: If not for your stupid balloon, I'd be on a plane... watching a woman do this... right now. But I'm not!
jokes.setup: Rachel's mounting frustration about missing her flight
jokes.punchline: Her specific, absurd complaint about missing airline safety demonstrations
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with specific visual detail. Shows how upset Rachel is by focusing on mundane airline details.

jokes.text: That's it! Enough with the keys. No one say keys!
jokes.setup: Continued argument about who had the keys
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's attempt to stop the word 'keys' from being said
jokes.explanation: Good escalation moment. Phoebe trying to control the argument through word prohibition is very her.

jokes.text: Why would I have the keys?!
jokes.setup: Phoebe just said not to say 'keys'
jokes.punchline: Joey immediately says 'keys' while making his point
jokes.explanation: Good immediate callback showing Joey's obliviousness to Phoebe's request.

jokes.text: Just as long as Phoebe gets her peas and onions, and Mario gets his Tots... It's my first Thanksgiving, and I...
jokes.setup: Monica's mounting frustration about trying to please everyone
jokes.punchline: Her emotional breakdown about her first Thanksgiving as host
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Monica's people-pleasing nature breaking down. Also calls Joey 'Mario' in her stress.

jokes.text: Monica, only dogs can hear you now. Look, the door's open. Here we go.
jokes.setup: Monica having an emotional breakdown with high-pitched crying
jokes.punchline: Joey's matter-of-fact observation about the frequency of her distress
jokes.explanation: Excellent Joey line that's both insensitive and oddly comforting. Great visual comedy implied.

jokes.text: Potatoes are ruined! Potatoes are ruined! Potatoes are ruined!
jokes.setup: Monica discovering the burnt dinner
jokes.punchline: Her repetitive, increasingly hysterical announcement
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Monica's perfectionist meltdown. The repetition emphasizes her panic.

jokes.text: You wanted lumps? Here you go, buddy. You got one!
jokes.setup: Monica in full meltdown mode about the ruined dinner
jokes.punchline: Aggressively serving Ross burnt potatoes with hostile satisfaction
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Monica's anger turning Ross's request into aggressive compliance.

jokes.text: We all had better plans. This was nobody's first choice!
jokes.setup: Rachel complaining about being stuck with them
jokes.punchline: Monica's harsh but honest assessment of everyone's situation
jokes.explanation: Brutally honest line that cuts through the pretense. Monica's hurt feelings coming out as harsh truth.

jokes.text: Now this feels like Thanksgiving.
jokes.setup: Everyone arguing and the dinner being ruined
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's zen observation about family dysfunction being traditional
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe line that reframes the disaster as authentically traditional. Great timing and wisdom.

jokes.text: He just sat in gravy.
jokes.setup: Watching Ugly Naked Guy through the window
jokes.punchline: Specific detail about his Thanksgiving dinner mishap
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag that adds to the Ugly Naked Guy running joke. Good specificity.

jokes.text: Ugly Naked Guy is having Thanksgiving dinner with Ugly Naked Gal.
jokes.setup: Continuing to watch their neighbor
jokes.punchline: Introduction of a female counterpart to their regular voyeurism target
jokes.explanation: Extends the running gag with a logical addition. Shows their neighbor isn't alone on Thanksgiving.

jokes.text: All right, Ugly Naked Guy! -Ugly naked dancing!
jokes.setup: Everyone watching their neighbor's Thanksgiving
jokes.punchline: Group encouragement and celebration of his dancing
jokes.explanation: Group participation in voyeurism becomes celebration. Shows their mood lifting.

jokes.text: It's nice that he has someone.
jokes.setup: Watching Ugly Naked Guy with his companion
jokes.punchline: Rachel's surprisingly sweet observation about loneliness
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment that shows Rachel's growth and empathy. Contrasts with earlier selfishness.

jokes.text: Who wants light cheese, and who wants dark cheese?
jokes.setup: Ross offering to carve what should be turkey
jokes.punchline: Reveals they're eating grilled cheese sandwiches instead
jokes.explanation: Great callback to Chandler's pathetic Thanksgiving meal that becomes their salvation. Perfect misdirection.

jokes.text: I don't even want to know about the dark cheese.
jokes.setup: Ross's offer of light vs dark cheese
jokes.punchline: Monica's suspicious response to the mysterious 'dark cheese'
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that plays with the absurdity of Ross's formal carving language for grilled cheese.

jokes.text: You got the bigger half! What did you wish for? -The bigger half.
jokes.setup: Monica and Joey breaking the wishbone
jokes.punchline: Joey's simple, practical wish that came true
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey response - simple, logical, and completely missing the point of wishing. Excellent economy and character work.

jokes.text: I think because it didn't involve divorce or projectile vomiting.
jokes.setup: Ross explaining why this Thanksgiving was great for him
jokes.punchline: Reference to Chandler's traumatic Thanksgiving story
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier story that shows Ross learned from Chandler's experience.

jokes.text: ...or if you didn't have syphilis and stuff...
jokes.setup: Ross listing why everyone's plans fell through
jokes.punchline: Casually including Joey's fake VD in the gratitude speech
jokes.explanation: Good callback that treats Joey's fake disease as real in the gratitude context. The 'and stuff' is a nice touch.

jokes.text: I'm very thankful that all of your Thanksgivings sucked.
jokes.setup: Ross's heartfelt gratitude speech about being together
jokes.punchline: His blunt summary that everyone's misfortune brought them together
jokes.explanation: Great punchline to the gratitude speech that's both heartfelt and hilariously honest. Perfect episode summary.

jokes.text: Here's to a lousy Christmas! -And a crappy New Year!
jokes.setup: Ross's toast about being thankful for everyone's bad luck
jokes.punchline: Extending the sentiment to future holidays with enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that shows everyone embracing the theme of finding joy in collective misfortune.
